title: These 3 Beaten-Down Growth Stocks Look Like Long-Term Comeback Candidates
author: John Ballard
description: Three consumer-focused growth stocks trading at discounts due to near-term macro headwinds present attractive long-term buying opportunities. Chewy benefits from a loyal customer base in a large pet industry with strong earnings growth expectations. Cava Group shows resilience with positive same-store sales and significant expansion runway compared to competitors. e.l.f. Beauty maintains market share gains and strong sales growth despite tariff pressures, with significant skincare expansion potential.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Trading at attractive 12x forward earnings with 32% annualized earnings growth expected. Strong customer loyalty (84% autoship sales), consistent net customer additions, and expansion into profitable pet healthcare services position it well for long-term growth despite near-term consumer spending pressure.

Demonstrating strong operational performance with 9.7% same-restaurant sales growth and positive comps for two consecutive years despite weak consumer environment. Significant expansion runway with only 459 locations versus Chipotle's 4,100+ locations, and 26% expected annual earnings growth supports long-term upside.

Seven consecutive years of market share gains with 25% net sales growth despite 22% year-to-date stock decline. Strong brand popularity, explosive skincare growth, and improving free cash flow ($190M trailing-12-month) suggest analyst expectations of 10% earnings growth are too conservative given expansion potential.
Mentioned as a comparable company with 4,100+ restaurants to highlight Cava's expansion opportunity. No direct investment recommendation or performance analysis provided in the article.

title: This Under-the-Radar Move Will Likely Drive e.l.f. Sales and Could Boost the Stock.
author: Geoffrey Seiler
description: e.l.f. Beauty unveiled new summer products for its Rhode brand, including three new items and limited-edition offerings. The expansion of Rhode's product assortment, combined with founder Hailey Bieber's social media promotion and higher profit margins, could drive significant sales growth. With a forward P/E of 16, the stock is considered undervalued with strong growth potential ahead.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The article highlights Rhode's product expansion as a significant growth driver, notes the brand's higher profit margins compared to e.l.f.'s main line, emphasizes strong social media promotion by Hailey Bieber with 58 million followers, and values the stock at a reasonable forward P/E of 16 with substantial upside potential from future distribution expansion.

title: Oil Jumps On Iran Strikes, Nasdaq 100 Falls On Hot Inflation: Stock Market Today
author: Piero Cingari
description: U.S. stocks fell broadly on Wednesday as inflation surged to 4.2% year-over-year and renewed U.S.-Iran tensions in the Strait of Hormuz drove oil prices up 3.3%. Technology stocks led losses amid valuation concerns, while energy stocks rallied. The Nasdaq 100 dropped 1.4%, the S&P 500 fell 0.9%, and the Dow declined 1.2%. Fed rate-hike odds increased following the inflation data.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Down 7.9% as industrials sector declined 2.5% due to broader market selloff and concerns over higher input costs from elevated oil prices
Down 4.5% as semiconductor stocks fell 2.9% amid technology sector weakness and concerns over elevated AI-related valuations
Surged 14.3% following strong fiscal Q4 earnings with diluted EPS up 66% year-over-year, offsetting broader market decline
Gained 6.5% as oil rally boosted energy sector; benefited from post-merger outlook, $8 billion buyback, and 33% dividend increase
Jumped 7.8% in relief bounce after quarterly report despite cautious guidance and potential $15-20M hit from higher oil prices; maintained bullish analyst ratings
Up 7.1% as one of the day's gainers, outperforming broader market decline
Rose 9.0% as crude spike and pump-price volatility promise to increase fuel margins; KeyBanc lifted price target to $600
Cratered 17.9% after announcing $7 billion equity financing for $39 billion AI-server orders, stoking dilution fears amid chip sector selloff
Slid 10.4% in sell-the-news reaction to ASCO data; H.C. Wainwright downgraded to Neutral and Bernstein reiterated Underperform
Down 3.5% as semiconductor sector declined 2.9% amid technology weakness
Led all sectors with 2.6% gain as crude surge and Middle East supply concerns boosted energy producers
Topped industry tables with 3.7% gain as oil prices jumped on Iran tensions
Down 3.8% as higher oil prices punished airlines through increased fuel costs
Tumbled 4.1% as rate-sensitive renewables slumped amid Fed rate-hike expectations
Fell 3.7% as gold extended sharp pullback, down 3.1% to $4,128/oz and down 13% for the month
Lost 2.9% as semiconductor sector weakened amid technology selloff and valuation concerns

title: 2 Growth Stocks Worth Buying Through the Volatility and Holding for a Lifetime
author: Micah Zimmerman
description: E.l.f. Beauty and Vita Coco are highlighted as durable growth stocks suitable for long-term holding. E.l.f. Beauty has delivered 29 consecutive quarters of double-digit net sales growth and is expanding through strategic brand acquisitions including Hailey Bieber's rhode brand. Vita Coco dominates the U.S. coconut water market with 51-52% share and is experiencing 37% year-over-year sales growth, with significant international expansion opportunities.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Company demonstrates consistent double-digit growth for 29 consecutive quarters, successful strategic acquisitions (Naturium and rhode), 25% year-over-year net sales growth, and expanding international presence outpacing legacy competitors like L'Oréal and Estée Lauder.

Strong Q1 2026 results with 37% year-over-year sales growth, operating margin expansion from 14.7% to 18.7%, market dominance with 51-52% U.S. coconut water share, minimal debt, $200 million cash position, and significant growth runway as market projected to expand from $5 billion to $19 billion by 2033.
Mentioned only as a reference point for e.l.f. Beauty's original consumer base; no specific analysis or performance data provided.
Characterized as a legacy beauty company struggling with single-digit growth in the same category where e.l.f. Beauty is achieving 25% growth, indicating competitive disadvantage.
Described as a legacy beauty company fighting to maintain single-digit growth, positioned as underperforming compared to e.l.f. Beauty's superior growth trajectory.

title: Why e.l.f. Beauty Stock Dropped13% in May
author: Jennifer Saibil
description: e.l.f. Beauty stock fell 13% in May despite beating earnings expectations with 35% revenue growth and strong performance from its Rhode luxury brand acquisition. The decline was driven by uncertainty around tariff impacts, which management expects to improve in 2027. The stock trades at a low valuation of 14x forward sales and is down 32% year-to-date.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Mixed signals: strong operational performance (35% revenue growth, 29 consecutive quarters of sales growth, Rhode brand success) and attractive valuation (14x forward sales) are offset by tariff uncertainty, unit sales declines from price increases, and ongoing market volatility. The stock fell despite beating earnings, indicating investor concern about forward guidance and tariff resolution timing.

Mentioned only as a platform where e.l.f. products are sold (38% unit sales increase on Amazon after price reduction). No direct sentiment applicable; mentioned for context of e.l.f.'s sales channels.
